evals.prompt: I need a single-page HR dashboard for our org's leadership. We have around 8 departments and roughly 400 employees. The top of the page should show a bar chart with average tenure by department — that's the most useful at-a-glance signal for us. Below that I want a section showing employee-level metrics for the selected department: a small chart with the distribution of compensation, another with performance ratings, and a sortable table listing the employees with their key fields. The key thing is: when I click a bar in the department chart, the employee section below should filter to just that department. I don't want a separate dropdown — clicking the bar IS the filter. Generate mock data for this.
evals.expected_output: A single-page Vizro dashboard implementing Pattern 2 (Single-Page Drill-Down): clicking a bar in the department chart filters the employee container.
expectations: Dashboard launches without errors and has no browser console errors
`import vizro.actions as va` appears in app.py
Department bar chart uses `va.set_control` with the Filter's `id` as `control` and `value='y'` (horizontal bar) or `value='x'` (vertical bar) matching the orientation
Bar chart is horizontal (orientation='h') — matches the project default for bar charts
Filter has an explicit `id` so `set_control` can reference it
Employee section is grouped inside a `vm.Container`, and the Filter is scoped to that container (either declared as a container control, or with explicit `targets`)
Department bar chart has a short action-oriented `header` (e.g. `header='Click a bar to filter by department'`)
No `filter_interaction` (deprecated API) anywhere in app.py
Spec file `spec/2_interaction_ux.yaml` includes an `interactions:` block referencing Pattern 2 / Single-Page Drill-Down, with source → control → targets documented

evals.prompt: We've got a two-page sales pipeline dashboard idea. Page 1 is the overview: KPIs, a couple of charts showing pipeline by region and win-rate trend, and a table listing all our sales reps with their key numbers. Page 2 is the rep deep-dive: KPIs for the selected rep, a chart showing their deals over time, a table of all their deals, and an export-data button at the bottom. The flow I want is: from Page 1, I click a row in the reps table, it navigates me to Page 2 with that rep already filtered in. From Page 2 there has to be a clear way back to Page 1 — I don't want users feeling stuck. Generate mock data for ~20 reps across 4 regions over the last 6 months.
evals.expected_output: Two-page Vizro dashboard implementing Pattern 1 (Hierarchical Drill-Down) with cross-page drill-through, back button, and data export.
expectations: Dashboard launches without errors and has no browser console errors
Page 1 reps table (AgGrid) uses `va.set_control` referencing a Filter on Page 2
Page 2 Filter has `show_in_url=True` — required for cross-page drill-through
Page 2 uses `layout=vm.Flex(direction='column')` at the page level (not a Grid) so the back button doesn't waste a full row
First component on Page 2 is a back `vm.Button` with `href='/<page-1-slug>'` and `variant='outlined'` — not an action; uses navigation
Page 2 includes a `vm.Button(text='Export data', actions=va.export_data())` (or similar)
Remaining Page 2 content (KPIs + charts + table) is wrapped in a `vm.Container` with its own `vm.Grid` layout for precise positioning
Page 1 reps table has a short action-oriented `header` (e.g. `header='Click a row to view rep details'`)
Spec file `spec/2_interaction_ux.yaml` includes an `interactions:` block referencing Pattern 1 / Hierarchical Drill-Down with `show_in_url: true` documented

evals.prompt: I'm analyzing health & wealth across countries. I want a single page with two charts side-by-side: on the left, a horizontal bar chart of life expectancy by country (filtered to the latest year), and on the right, a bump chart that shows how each country's GDP rank has changed over the last 50 years. The bump chart will have a lot of overlapping lines — that's expected. I want to be able to click a country in the bar chart and have that country's line in the bump chart light up — bolder, brighter — while the others fade into the background. I don't want clicking to filter the data out, just to highlight. Use the gapminder dataset.
evals.expected_output: Single-page dashboard implementing Pattern 3 (Comparison Spotlight) — cross-highlight from bar chart into a custom bump chart.
expectations: Dashboard launches without errors and has no browser console errors
Bump chart is implemented as a custom `@capture('graph')` function with a `highlight_*=None` kwarg (exact name is the agent's choice — `highlight_country`, `selected_country`, etc.)

Inside the custom chart, non-highlighted traces are visually de-emphasized (reduced opacity and/or thinner lines) and the matching trace is emphasized (full opacity / bolder line) when a country is set. Exact values (e.g. `opacity=0.3` vs `0.2`, `line_width=3` vs `2`) are not load-bearing — only the visual contrast is
A `vm.Parameter` targets the custom chart's highlight kwarg (target string of the form `<chart_id>.<highlight_kwarg>`) and has `visible=False`
The Parameter's selector includes a 'no highlight' sentinel option (typically the string `'NONE'`) so users can reset to no highlight
Bar chart uses `va.set_control` referencing the Parameter's `id` with `value='y'` (since bar is horizontal)
No Filter is involved — this is cross-parameter, not cross-filter (data should NOT be removed, only styled)
Bar chart has an action-oriented `header` (e.g. `header='Click a bar to highlight that country in the chart'`)
Spec file `spec/2_interaction_ux.yaml` includes an `interactions:` block referencing Pattern 3 / Comparison Spotlight with `visible: false` documented

evals.prompt: I'm building an appointments dashboard for a clinic. We want to see appointment density across the week. The main visual is a heatmap with day-of-week on one axis and time slot on the other axis (15-minute slots from 8am to 6pm), and the cells colored by number of appointments. Below the heatmap there should be a table listing individual appointments. When the user clicks a cell in the heatmap — say Tuesday at 10:00 — the table should filter to show only Tuesday-10:00 appointments. The clinic admin also needs to be able to download whatever appointments are currently shown in the table as a CSV — so they can hand the day's schedule to a doctor or pass it to billing. Generate mock data for a couple of weeks of appointments across 5 doctors.
evals.expected_output: Single-page dashboard combining Pattern 4 (Multi-Dimensional Slice — heatmap click sets two filters via an actions chain) AND Pattern 5 (Data Export — button-triggered CSV download of the filtered table).
expectations: Dashboard launches without errors and has no browser console errors
Heatmap (Graph) has an `actions=[...]` list with two `va.set_control` calls — one for the day filter (`value='x'` or `value='y'` based on axis), one for the time-slot filter
Two separate Filter components are declared, one per dimension, each with an explicit `id`
Both Filters target the appointments table (via `targets=[...]` or by being scoped to the table's container)
Heatmap uses Vizro default color palette (no explicit `color_continuous_scale='Blues'` or similar) — consistent with the project color rule
Heatmap has an action-oriented `header` (e.g. `header='Click a cell to filter the appointments below'`)
Table is an AG Grid (for sortable inspection of underlying records)
Page includes a `vm.Button` with `actions=va.export_data()` — built-in action, NOT wrapped in `vm.Action`
Export button text is clear and action-oriented (e.g. 'Export data', 'Download CSV') — not generic like 'Button' or 'Submit'
Export button is placed sensibly relative to the data it exports (near the appointments table or at the bottom of the page) — not floating in an unrelated spot
No `filter_interaction` or other deprecated APIs

Spec file `spec/2_interaction_ux.yaml` includes an `interactions:` block referencing BOTH Pattern 4 / Multi-Dimensional Slice (heatmap → two filters actions chain) AND Pattern 5 / Data Export (the button)
